risks.title: Instruction injection and priority override
risks.definition: Untrusted or lower-priority text can be interpreted as instructions that override the intended instruction hierarchy.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify explicit override markers and obfuscating control characters; cannot prove model compliance.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May judge whether cited text conflicts with instruction hierarchy; cannot observe target-model behavior.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May report observed bypass under a versioned attack set and model configuration.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Not generally applicable to prompt-only behavior.

risks.title: Incomplete or unresolved prompt template
risks.definition: A prompt is empty or retains unresolved template placeholders, preventing the intended contract from being instantiated.
risks.verityOriginalRationale: Prompt build-integrity risk needed for deterministic preflight; not represented as a security vulnerability by the selected external taxonomies.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove empty content or supported unresolved placeholder syntax.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May identify materially missing context beyond known syntax.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May observe unusable or unstable outputs caused by missing context.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Not applicable.

risks.title: Sensitive information embedded in prompts
risks.definition: Credentials, secrets, personal data, or other sensitive values are embedded in prompt material exposed to models or users.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify supported secret patterns and sensitive markers; cannot determine all contextual sensitivity.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May classify contextual sensitivity only on sanitized evidence.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May test whether supplied secrets can be extracted from a target model context.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Not applicable to prompt-only review.

knownGaps: Prompt path only recognizes a synthetic fixture token

risks.title: Contradictory instructions or assignments
risks.definition: Instructions, constraints, examples, or assigned values cannot be satisfied together without violating part of the prompt.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove narrowly structured conflicting assignments.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May judge cited instruction pairs, subject to evidence and validation policy.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May measure unstable behavior caused by conflicts.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Not applicable.

knownGaps: Semantic extractor compares only adjacent non-empty lines

risks.title: Hidden or deceptive text encoding
risks.definition: Control characters, bidirectional overrides, invisible text, or encoding tricks obscure the effective instructions from reviewers.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove the presence of supported control and bidirectional characters.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: Should not be the primary detector for byte/encoding facts.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May test whether encoded instructions alter behavior.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe decoded runtime artifacts for Skills.

risks.title: Missing or ambiguous output contract
risks.definition: A prompt requests structured or constrained output without defining sufficient fields, schema, validation, or failure behavior.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May parse declared output schemas and prove structural inconsistencies when a supported contract exists.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May judge whether a requested format lacks material field requirements.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May measure schema adherence and failure handling.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Not applicable.

risks.title: Excessive tool authorization
risks.definition: Tool or capability authorization is broader than the task requires or uses open-ended wildcard grants.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove supported wildcard grants and compare structured declarations.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May judge whether declared tools are necessary for stated goals.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May test whether a prompt induces unauthorized tool requests.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe actual attempted capability use under policy.

risks.title: Untrusted content boundary is undefined
risks.definition: External, retrieved, tool-produced, or user-supplied data is not clearly separated from trusted instructions.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May detect declared external sources and missing supported delimiters but cannot prove model interpretation.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May judge whether cited data and instruction regions are ambiguously separated.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May inject controlled content through each trust boundary and observe behavior.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe how a Skill propagates retrieved content into tools and prompts.

risks.title: Confabulation and unverifiable claims
risks.definition: A prompt encourages factual or consequential claims without requiring sources, uncertainty, or verification appropriate to the use case.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: Can identify only explicit absence/presence of supported verification clauses; absence alone is not proof of unsafe behavior.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess whether verification requirements are appropriate to stated consequential claims.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May measure unsupported claims against a controlled factual set.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Not applicable.

risks.title: Skill package and manifest specification nonconformance
risks.definition: The Skill lacks required structure or its SKILL.md frontmatter violates the supported Agent Skills specification.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove supported structural and schema violations against a named specification version.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess description quality but should not replace schema validation.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe compatibility failures but not redefine specification conformance.

knownGaps: Current name syntax is looser than the official specification

risks.title: Unsafe or broken local resource references
risks.definition: A Skill references absent resources, escapes the package root, uses unsafe path forms, or creates ambiguous reference resolution.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove missing files and supported unsafe path forms within the immutable snapshot.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: Not the primary detector for path facts.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe attempted path access under containment.

risks.title: Dependency drift, known vulnerabilities, or unverifiable provenance
risks.definition: Dependencies or packaged artifacts are floating, vulnerable, untrusted, or lack verifiable source/build provenance.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove declared versions are unpinned and inspect lock/provenance artifacts; known-vulnerability claims require a trusted database snapshot.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: Should not invent dependency vulnerability facts.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe installation/runtime behavior but cannot prove supply-chain provenance alone.

risks.title: Overbroad declared permissions or capabilities
risks.definition: Declared file, process, network, credential, or tool capabilities exceed the least privilege needed for stated behavior.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove wildcard grants and compare normalized declarations to observed static capability facts.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May judge necessity relative to description using cited capability evidence.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable to Skill execution.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe attempted capabilities and policy denials.

risks.title: Untrusted external instructions or tool content
risks.definition: A Skill or agent fetches external content and treats it as trusted instructions without provenance, validation, or isolation.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify explicit runtime-fetch declarations and supported untrusted data flows.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May judge declaration/instruction trust mismatch on cited evidence.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable to Skill execution.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May inject controlled external/tool content and observe propagation.

knownGaps: Only custom manifest modes are recognized

risks.title: Dangerous command or dynamic code execution
risks.definition: Skill code constructs or invokes shell commands, interpreters, or dynamic code in a way that may permit unintended execution.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May prove dangerous call forms and, with data-flow analysis, supported attacker-controlled flows; pattern presence alone is not exploitability proof.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess declaration mismatch but must not replace AST/data-flow facts.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe attempted process execution under isolation.

knownGaps: No JavaScript/TypeScript or robust Shell parser

risks.title: Unsafe deserialization or parser configuration
risks.definition: Skill code deserializes untrusted data using mechanisms that can instantiate objects, execute code, or bypass intended parser safety.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify known unsafe parser/deserializer APIs and supported data flows.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess whether data is described as untrusted but cannot replace call analysis.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe deserialization effects with controlled payloads.

risks.title: Weak cryptography or transport protection
risks.definition: Skill code uses obsolete cryptographic primitives or sends protected information over transport lacking required security.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify known weak APIs and insecure URL/verification configuration.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess declared sensitivity but should not determine cryptographic correctness.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe connections but cannot prove cryptographic design correctness alone.

knownGaps: Only one selected weak-hash Bandit check

risks.title: Untrusted network destination and SSRF
risks.definition: Untrusted input or configuration can cause requests to unintended, internal, local, metadata, or unsafe-scheme destinations.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify risky URL APIs/schemes and supported source-to-request flows.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess whether declared network scope matches behavior.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe/deny controlled network attempts and DNS/redirect behavior.

risks.title: Unsafe output rendering or downstream handling
risks.definition: Generated, retrieved, or user-controlled output reaches templates, commands, browsers, or tools without context-appropriate validation or escaping.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify known unsafe sinks/configurations and supported source-to-sink flows.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May classify intended output use on cited evidence.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May generate controlled outputs for downstream safety assertions.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe actual rendering/tool effects under isolation.

risks.title: Hardcoded or exposed credentials
risks.definition: Credentials, API keys, passwords, or tokens are embedded in or exposed by the Skill package or its configuration.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify supported secret/password patterns with redaction; absence is not proof that no secret exists.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: Must not receive known secrets and is not the primary credential detector.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: May test extraction only with fake credentials and explicit budget.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe access to fake credentials under isolation.

risks.title: Declared behavior differs from implementation
risks.definition: Manifest description, compatibility, permissions, or instructions materially understate, overstate, or conflict with statically observed implementation capabilities.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May build normalized declaration and capability facts but cannot judge every semantic mismatch.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May confirm controlled mismatch types using cited declaration and implementation evidence.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May compare actual attempted behavior with declarations.

knownGaps: Capability facts exist but no deterministic declaration-to-fact comparison
Only Python AST and manifest facts
Semantic extractor uses only first Python file and bounded excerpts

risks.title: Cross-file or cross-language unsafe data flow
risks.definition: Untrusted data crosses files, modules, processes, or language boundaries and reaches a privileged sink without adequate validation.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: Requires language-aware call/data-flow analysis with explicit supported-language and source/sink matrices.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May prioritize or explain evidence but cannot substitute for data-flow facts.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May observe flows/effects that static analysis cannot resolve.

risks.title: Weak runtime isolation and host escape
risks.definition: A Skill can access host resources, credentials, processes, or networks beyond a one-shot least-privilege execution boundary.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May identify capability requests and dangerous APIs but cannot prove runtime containment.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess stated isolation requirements but cannot test them.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Must enforce and test one-shot isolation, fake credentials, resource limits, network policy, and destruction.

risks.title: MCP authorization and session boundary failure
risks.definition: MCP authorization, tokens, redirects, consent, sessions, or proxy behavior violate audience, client, or user trust boundaries.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: May validate structured MCP configuration and known insecure implementation patterns when MCP intake exists.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: May assess documented trust boundaries but not prove protocol correctness.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Not applicable to Prompt black-box testing.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: May exercise controlled MCP interactions only inside the approved runtime boundary.

risks.title: Incomplete or misleading audit coverage
risks.definition: A review or report implies safety when relevant checks did not run, failed, do not support the artifact, or cover only narrow subcases.

layerBoundaries.L0_static: Must expose plan/execution/coverage independently from findings and capability breadth.
layerBoundaries.L1_semantic: Must expose Provider/model/validation failure and never convert failure to no finding.
layerBoundaries.V1_5_blackbox: Must expose test-set/model/budget coverage and unexecuted cases.
layerBoundaries.V2_sandbox: Must expose runtime policy, blocked operations, teardown, and unobserved behavior.

knownGaps: Execution status UI can still be mistaken for breadth
